description.task: Implement naive pattern matching (brute-force string search). Slide a window of length m across the text of length n and check for character-by-character match at each position. Return all starting indices where the pattern occurs.
instructions: Inner loop: int j=0; while(j<m&&text.charAt(i+j)==pattern.charAt(j)) j++; if(j==m) positions.add(i);
Or use String.regionMatches or substring check instead
Naive search is O(n*m) — inefficient for large texts with repeated patterns

description.task: Implement the Knuth-Morris-Pratt (KMP) string search algorithm. First, build the Longest Prefix Suffix (LPS) / failure function array, then use it to skip unnecessary comparisons during the search.
instructions: Mismatch handling: if(j!=0) j=lps[j-1]; else i++;
KMP is O(n+m) — far more efficient than O(n*m) naive search
KMP finds AABA at [0,9,12] same as naive but without backtracking text pointer
ABABAB occurs at [0,2,7,9] in the second test

description.task: Find all starting positions in text where any anagram (permutation) of the pattern occurs. Use a sliding window of size m and compare character frequency counts. This achieves O(n) time.
instructions: cbaebabacd with pattern abc: anagrams at index 0 (cba) and index 6 (bac)
abab with pattern ab: anagrams at 0 (ab) and 2 (ab)
